articles.title: Horrified Wagga residents call for proper sanitation at homeless camp where baby was found dead
articles.description: Tent where twins were born up to a 15-minute walk away from nearest public toilets or running water Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The tent where a newborn baby was found dead at Wagga beach at the weekend was part of a homeless encampment up to a 15-minute walk away from the nearest public toilets or running water, with residents in a nearby apartment block saying conditions were “worse than a Syrian war camp”. The tragedy has prompted fury in the community, with residents of Wagga Wagga calling on authorities to take urgent action to make the encampments safe and sanitary. Continue reading...

articles.title: Horoscope for Wednesday, May 06, 2026
articles.description:
Moon Alert

There are no restrictions to shopping or important decisions today. The moon is in Capricorn.

Aries (March 21-April 19)

A so-so day

Be wise and don't challenge authority figures today, especially parents, bosses, supervisors, the government and the police. You won't win. This is definitely the day to think for yourself, especially about choosing your battles. (Remember — you hate to lose.)

Taurus (April 20-May 20)

An average day

This is a tense day for discussions about controversial subjects like politics, religion and racial issues. You might also be challenged in situations related to the law or medicine. Fortunately, the sun and Mercury in your sign will balance things. Go gently.

Gemini (May 21-June 20)

An average day

Financial discussions will definitely present a squeeze play for you today. Very likely, someone older, more experienced, or in authority will tell you why something can't be done. Do your planning but play your cards close to your vest.

Cancer (June 21-July 22)

A so-so day

Relations with spouses, partners and close friends might be strained today because people are critical or cold. This makes you feel out of touch with others. (And you are a sensitive sign.) Let this go. This is a fleeting dark cloud on your horizon.

Leo (July 23-Aug. 22)

An average day

Health issues, as well as challenges at work, might arise today because someone is critical or uncooperative. (At least, that's how it feels to you.) Avoid controversial subjects because they will be a bummer. Someone might quit today. Stay busy and stay positive.

Virgo (Aug. 23-Sept. 22)

An average day

This is a tough day for relating to your kids and for romantic relationships because the vibes are negative. (Please note: Things look worse than they really are.) People feel disconnected or disapproving of each other. Your perspective is distorted today. Relax.

Libra (Sept. 23-Oct. 22)

A so-so day

You like to get along with others, which is why this might feel like a challenging, perhaps even gloomy, day for you. People might not be particularly friendly, or perhaps you might have to say goodbye to someone. There's a discouraging failure of communication.

Scorpio (Oct. 23-Nov. 21)

A so-so day

Be careful about falling into negative thinking about yourself today. You might be inclined to downgrade yourself in the eyes of others or dwell on negative experiences from the past. This is not real. Things look worse than they are today. If you work hard, you’ll feel better.

Sagittarius (Nov. 22-Dec. 21)

An average day

Financial issues look tight today, which is why spending money on pleasure outings or for your kids seems questionable. ("Should I do this?") You would rather save your money. In addition, your kids might feel like an increased responsibility. The truth is that things are actually better than they look today. (You're wearing your sad glasses.)

Capricorn (Dec. 22-Jan. 19)

A so-so day

Life looks worse than it is today. Keep this in mind and remind yourself to stay positive — because you can. Sometimes happiness is a state of mind — it's a choice. Nevertheless, you might feel hurt, slighted or separated from a family member today. (Things will look better by Friday.)

Aquarius (Jan. 20-Feb. 18)

An average day

Home and family are your focus, and today you want to socialize. Nevertheless, for some reason, you feel cut off from others, which is why you might seek solitude. Don't listen to criticism because it's not real. Things look worse than they are today. Courage!

Pisces (Feb. 19-March 20)

An average day

You might be disappointed in a friend today. They might criticize you or fail to respond to your needs. You might even feel cut off from someone. Possibly, this relates to financial matters, money, or a possession. Let it go because this is a temporary, negative confusion today.

If today is your birthday

Actress Adrianne Palicki (1983) shares your birthday today. You're in tune with the emotions of others. You're strong-minded and willing to help. This year your creativity, zest and desire for life are at a pinnacle. Enjoy socializing. Nurture your relationships and the happiness and beauty you have within. Let your guard down and loosen up a bit.

articles.title: Bangladesh Red Crescent Society Situation Report 1: Northeastern Haor Region Flash Flood (30 April 2026)
articles.description: Country: Bangladesh Source: Bangladesh Red Crescent Society Please refer to the attached file. Overall Situation: Heavy to very heavy rainfall across the northeastern region and upstream catchments has caused a rapid rise in water levels in major river systems, including the Surma–Kushiyara, Khoai, and Dhanu–Baulai basins. As of 30 April 2026, the Flood Forecasting and Warning Centre (FFWC) reported 1 river station under severe flood condition, 4 river stations under flood status, and 4 stations under warning alert. The situation remains dynamic, with heightened risk of flash flooding in low-lying haor areas. The most affected districts currently include Moulvibazar, Sylhet, Habiganj, and Netrokona, while Sunamganj, Kishoreganj, Cumilla, Brahmanbaria, and Feni remain under close observation. Approximately 3 million people across 5 districts, 17 Upazilas, and 102 Unions are considered at risk if rainfall intensity continues. The agricultural sector is already facing impacts, particularly standing boro paddy nearing harvest. BDRCS has activated the Emergency Operations Centre. All 9 affected district units are on standby with trained volunteers, response teams, and pre-positioned relief items ready for immediate deployment. Coordination with government authorities and humanitarian partners is ongoing to ensure timely preparedness and response actions.
